questions.question: An array A contains n integers in non-decreasing order: A[1] <= A[2] <= ... <= A[n]. Describe, using Pascal-like pseudo code, a linear time algorithm to find i, j such that A[i] + A[j] = M, given integer M, if such i, j exist.
questions.answer: Use two pointers: left = 1, right = n. While left < right: if A[left] + A[right] = M then found; if A[left] + A[right] < M then left := left + 1; else right := right - 1. If left >= right and not found, no such i,j exist.
questions.to_find: A linear-time algorithm (Pascal-like pseudocode) to find indices i, j such that A[i] + A[j] = M in a sorted array.

understand.plain: We have a sorted array of integers. We need to find two positions i and j such that the values at those positions add up to a target M. We must do this in O(n) time, meaning one pass through the data. The trick is to use two pointers starting from both ends and moving inward.

keywords.explain: A method that uses two indices starting at opposite ends of a sorted array, moving toward each other, to find a pair with a desired property in O(n) time.
keywords.example: Array [1,3,5,7,9], M=10: left=1(val=1), right=5(val=9), sum=10 — found!

keywords.explain: Each element is >= the previous one; the array is sorted in ascending (or equal) fashion.

understand.visual_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 520 160'><style>.bg{fill:var(--cell-bg,#F1EFE8);stroke:var(--cell-line,#B4B2A9);stroke-width:2;}.hi{fill:var(--hi-bg,#CECBF6);stroke:var(--hi-line,#7F77DD);stroke-width:3;}.ok{fill:var(--ok,#1D9E75);}.no{fill:var(--no,#D85A30);}.ink{fill:var(--color-text-primary,#2C2C2A);}.mut{fill:var(--mut,#5F5E5A);}.big{font-size:26px;font-weight:700;}.lbl{font-size:18px;font-weight:600;}.sub{font-size:14px;}.tag{font-size:14px;font-weight:600;}text{font-family:system-ui,-apple-system,Segoe UI,sans-serif;}</style><rect x='10' y='30' width='70' height='50' class='hi'/><rect x='80' y='30' width='70' height='50' class='bg'/><rect x='150' y='30' width='70' height='50' class='bg'/><rect x='220' y='30' width='70' height='50' class='bg'/><rect x='290' y='30' width='70' height='50' class='bg'/><rect x='360' y='30' width='70' height='50' class='hi'/><text x='45' y='62' class='lbl ink' text-anchor='middle'>1</text><text x='115' y='62' class='lbl ink' text-anchor='middle'>3</text><text x='185' y='62' class='lbl ink' text-anchor='middle'>5</text><text x='255' y='62' class='lbl ink' text-anchor='middle'>7</text><text x='325' y='62' class='lbl ink' text-anchor='middle'>8</text><text x='395' y='62' class='lbl ink' text-anchor='middle'>9</text><text x='45' y='100' class='sub mut' text-anchor='middle'>left</text><text x='395' y='100' class='sub mut' text-anchor='middle'>right</text><text x='260' y='140' class='sub ink' text-anchor='middle'>Move left right if sum < M; move right left if sum > M</text></svg>
understand.visual_alt: Sorted array with left pointer at index 1 and right pointer at index n, arrows showing they move toward each other.

given.aim: Find indices i and j in a sorted array such that A[i] + A[j] = M, in O(n) time.

terms.connects: We compare A[left] + A[right] against M to decide pointer movement

terms.meaning: Two pointer indices starting at 1 and n respectively
terms.connects: Core mechanism: shrink range based on current sum vs M

given.plan: Maintain two pointers at the two ends of the array. If their sum equals M, we found the answer. If the sum is less than M, increment left to increase the sum. If greater, decrement right. Repeat until pointers meet.

steps.title: Initialize two pointers
steps.apply: Set left to the first index and right to the last index of the array.
steps.note: This works because the array is sorted — increasing left increases the sum, decreasing right decreases the sum.

steps.title: Iterate and adjust pointers
steps.apply:
Pseudocode:
left := 1;
right := n;
found := false;
while (left < right) and (not found) do
  if A[left] + A[right] = M then
    found := true
  else if A[left] + A[right] < M then
    left := left + 1
  else
    right := right - 1
  end
end;
if found then
  writeln('i=', left, ' j=', right)
else
  writeln('No such i, j exist')
steps.note: Each iteration moves at least one pointer, so at most n-1 iterations — O(n) time.

solution.result: The two-pointer algorithm runs in O(n) time. At each step, A[left]+A[right] is compared to M: if equal, found; if less, left++; if greater, right--.

questions.formula_note: Two-pointer (or meet-in-the-middle on sorted array) technique: exploits sorted order to solve pair-sum in O(n) by converging from both ends.

questions.question:
An array A contains n >= 1 positive integers in the locations A[0], A[1], ..., A[n-1]. The following program fragment prints the length of a shortest sequence of consecutive elements of A, such that the sum of their values is >= M, a given positive number. It prints 'n+1' if no such sequence exists. Complete the program by filling in the boxes.

begin
  i := 0;
  j := 0;
  sum := A[0];
  min_len := n + 1;
  while j < n do
  begin
    if sum < M then
    begin
      j := j + 1;
      if j < n then sum := sum + A[j]
    end
    else
    begin
      if [ ] < min_len then min_len := [ ];
      sum := sum - A[i];
      i := i + 1
    end
  end;
  writeln(min_len)
end

The two boxes [ ] should be filled with:
A. (j - i + 1) and (j - i + 1)
B. (j - i) and (j - i)
C. (j - i + 1) and (j - i)
D. (j - i) and (j - i + 1)
questions.to_find: The correct expressions to fill the two blanks in the sliding-window program that finds the shortest subarray with sum >= M.

understand.plain: We have an array of positive integers and a target M. We want the shortest contiguous subarray whose sum is at least M. The given code uses a sliding window approach: expand the window by moving j right when sum < M, and shrink it from the left by moving i right when sum >= M. When sum >= M, we need to record the current window length.

keywords.explain: A technique using two pointers (i = left, j = right) to maintain a subarray, expanding or shrinking it based on a condition.
The number of elements from index i to index j (inclusive) is j - i + 1.
keywords.example: i=2, j=4: elements at indices 2,3,4 = 3 elements = 4-2+1 = 3.

understand.visual_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 500 130'><style>.bg{fill:var(--cell-bg,#F1EFE8);stroke:var(--cell-line,#B4B2A9);stroke-width:2;}.hi{fill:var(--hi-bg,#CECBF6);stroke:var(--hi-line,#7F77DD);stroke-width:3;}.ok{fill:var(--ok,#1D9E75);}.no{fill:var(--no,#D85A30);}.ink{fill:var(--color-text-primary,#2C2C2A);}.mut{fill:var(--mut,#5F5E5A);}.big{font-size:26px;font-weight:700;}.lbl{font-size:18px;font-weight:600;}.sub{font-size:14px;}.tag{font-size:14px;font-weight:600;}text{font-family:system-ui,-apple-system,Segoe UI,sans-serif;}</style><rect x='10' y='30' width='60' height='50' class='bg'/><rect x='70' y='30' width='60' height='50' class='bg'/><rect x='130' y='30' width='60' height='50' class='hi'/><rect x='190' y='30' width='60' height='50' class='hi'/><rect x='250' y='30' width='60' height='50' class='hi'/><rect x='310' y='30' width='60' height='50' class='bg'/><text x='40' y='62' class='lbl ink' text-anchor='middle'>A[0]</text><text x='100' y='62' class='lbl ink' text-anchor='middle'>A[1]</text><text x='160' y='62' class='lbl ink' text-anchor='middle'>A[2]</text><text x='220' y='62' class='lbl ink' text-anchor='middle'>A[3]</text><text x='280' y='62' class='lbl ink' text-anchor='middle'>A[4]</text><text x='340' y='62' class='lbl ink' text-anchor='middle'>A[5]</text><text x='160' y='100' class='sub mut' text-anchor='middle'>i</text><text x='280' y='100' class='sub mut' text-anchor='middle'>j</text><text x='250' y='125' class='sub ink' text-anchor='middle'>window length = j - i + 1 = 4 - 2 + 1 = 3</text></svg>
understand.visual_alt: Array with a highlighted window from index i to index j, showing window length = j - i + 1.

given.aim: Find the correct window-length expression for the sliding window algorithm.

terms.meaning: Left boundary of the current window (inclusive)
terms.example: i=2 means window starts at A[2]
terms.connects: Incremented to shrink window from the left when sum >= M

terms.meaning: Right boundary of the current window (inclusive)
terms.example: j=5 means window ends at A[5]
terms.connects: Incremented to expand window when sum < M

terms.meaning: Tracks the length of the shortest valid window found so far
terms.connects: Updated whenever sum >= M and current window is shorter

given.plan: When sum >= M, the current window A[i..j] is a candidate. Its length is j - i + 1. We compare this to min_len and update if smaller. Both blank expressions refer to the same quantity: the current window length.

steps.title: Determine current window length
steps.apply: The window spans from index i to index j inclusive. Number of elements = j - i + 1. For example, i=1, j=3: indices 1,2,3 = 3 elements = 3-1+1 = 3.
steps.note: A common off-by-one error is using j-i instead of j-i+1; j-i gives the count excluding one endpoint.

steps.title: Fill both blanks with (j - i + 1)
steps.formula_raw: if (j - i + 1) < min_len then min_len := (j - i + 1)
steps.apply: First blank: condition check — is current window length less than min_len? Answer: (j - i + 1). Second blank: assignment — update min_len with current window length. Answer: (j - i + 1). Both blanks are the same expression.
steps.note: Option A is correct: (j - i + 1) and (j - i + 1).

questions.formula_note: Sliding window on positive integer array; window length from index i to j inclusive = j - i + 1.

questions.question:
Let A be a two-dimensional array declared as follows:
A: array [1..10][1..15] of integer;
Assuming that each integer takes one memory location, the array is stored in row-major order and the first element of the array is stored at location 100, what is the address of the element A[i][j]?

A. 15i + j + 84
B. 15j + i + 84
C. 10i + j + 89
D. 10j + i + 89
questions.to_find: Address of element A[i][j] in a 2D array stored in row-major order with base address 100.

understand.plain: A 2D array A has rows 1 to 10 and columns 1 to 15. It is stored row by row in memory (row-major). The first element A[1][1] is at address 100. We need to figure out the memory address of any element A[i][j] by counting how many elements come before it.

keywords.explain: 2D array stored row by row: all elements of row 1, then row 2, etc.
Base address + number of elements before the target element (each element takes 1 memory location).
keywords.example: A[2][3]: 1 full row (15 elements) + 2 more = 17 elements before it; address = 100 + 17 = 117.

understand.visual_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 500 160'><style>.bg{fill:var(--cell-bg,#F1EFE8);stroke:var(--cell-line,#B4B2A9);stroke-width:2;}.hi{fill:var(--hi-bg,#CECBF6);stroke:var(--hi-line,#7F77DD);stroke-width:3;}.ok{fill:var(--ok,#1D9E75);}.no{fill:var(--no,#D85A30);}.ink{fill:var(--color-text-primary,#2C2C2A);}.mut{fill:var(--mut,#5F5E5A);}.big{font-size:26px;font-weight:700;}.lbl{font-size:18px;font-weight:600;}.sub{font-size:14px;}.tag{font-size:14px;font-weight:600;}text{font-family:system-ui,-apple-system,Segoe UI,sans-serif;}</style><rect x='10' y='10' width='480' height='40' class='bg'/><rect x='10' y='50' width='480' height='40' class='bg'/><rect x='10' y='90' width='200' height='40' class='hi'/><rect x='210' y='90' width='280' height='40' class='bg'/><text x='250' y='36' class='sub ink' text-anchor='middle'>Row 1: A[1][1] ... A[1][15]  (15 elements, addr 100..114)</text><text x='250' y='76' class='sub ink' text-anchor='middle'>Row 2: A[2][1] ... A[2][15]  (15 elements, addr 115..129)</text><text x='105' y='116' class='sub ink' text-anchor='middle'>A[i][1]..A[i][j] (j elements)</text><text x='355' y='116' class='sub mut' text-anchor='middle'>rest of row i</text><text x='250' y='150' class='sub ink' text-anchor='middle'>Address = 100 + 15*(i-1) + (j-1) = 15i + j + 84</text></svg>
understand.visual_alt: Memory layout of 2D array in row-major order: each row occupies 15 consecutive locations, A[i][j] offset = 15*(i-1)+(j-1) from base 100.

given.aim: Calculate the memory address of A[i][j] in row-major 2D array with given dimensions and base.

terms.meaning: Address of A[1][1], the first element
terms.connects: All other addresses are offsets from this
In row-major, to reach row i we skip (i-1)*15 elements

terms.meaning: Number of elements before A[i][j] from A[1][1]

given.plan: Count (i-1) complete rows before row i, each with 15 elements, plus (j-1) elements in row i before column j. Add base address 100.

steps.title: Compute offset from A[1][1] to A[i][j]
steps.apply: (i-1) complete rows each with 15 elements = 15(i-1) elements, plus (j-1) elements in the current row. Offset = 15(i-1) + (j-1) = 15i - 15 + j - 1 = 15i + j - 16.
steps.note: Indices start at 1, so we subtract 1 from each to get 0-based offset.

questions.formula_note: Row-major address formula: Address(A[i][j]) = Base + (i - row_start) * num_cols + (j - col_start), where indices start at 1.

questions.question:
An n x n array v is defined as follows:
v[i][j] = i - j  for all i, j where 1 <= i, j <= n
The sum of the elements of the array v is

A. 0
B. n - 1
C. n^2 - 3n + 2
D. n^2(n-1)/2
questions.to_find: The sum of all elements of the n x n array v where v[i][j] = i - j.

understand.plain: We have an n x n matrix where every entry at row i, column j is equal to i minus j. We want to add up all n^2 entries. The key insight is that the matrix is antisymmetric: v[i][j] = -v[j][i], so each pair of off-diagonal elements cancels, and diagonal elements are all zero.

keywords.explain: A matrix where element (i,j) = -(element (j,i)). All diagonal entries are 0.
For every pair (i,j) with i != j, (i-j) + (j-i) = 0. Diagonal entries v[i][i] = i-i = 0.

understand.visual_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 360 200'><style>.bg{fill:var(--cell-bg,#F1EFE8);stroke:var(--cell-line,#B4B2A9);stroke-width:2;}.hi{fill:var(--hi-bg,#CECBF6);stroke:var(--hi-line,#7F77DD);stroke-width:3;}.ok{fill:var(--ok,#1D9E75);}.no{fill:var(--no,#D85A30);}.ink{fill:var(--color-text-primary,#2C2C2A);}.mut{fill:var(--mut,#5F5E5A);}.big{font-size:26px;font-weight:700;}.lbl{font-size:18px;font-weight:600;}.sub{font-size:14px;}.tag{font-size:14px;font-weight:600;}text{font-family:system-ui,-apple-system,Segoe UI,sans-serif;}</style><rect x='80' y='20' width='60' height='50' class='hi'/><rect x='140' y='20' width='60' height='50' class='bg'/><rect x='200' y='20' width='60' height='50' class='bg'/><rect x='80' y='70' width='60' height='50' class='bg'/><rect x='140' y='70' width='60' height='50' class='hi'/><rect x='200' y='70' width='60' height='50' class='bg'/><rect x='80' y='120' width='60' height='50' class='bg'/><rect x='140' y='120' width='60' height='50' class='bg'/><rect x='200' y='120' width='60' height='50' class='hi'/><text x='110' y='52' class='lbl ink' text-anchor='middle'>0</text><text x='170' y='52' class='lbl ink' text-anchor='middle'>-1</text><text x='230' y='52' class='lbl ink' text-anchor='middle'>-2</text><text x='110' y='102' class='lbl ink' text-anchor='middle'>1</text><text x='170' y='102' class='lbl ink' text-anchor='middle'>0</text><text x='230' y='102' class='lbl ink' text-anchor='middle'>-1</text><text x='110' y='152' class='lbl ink' text-anchor='middle'>2</text><text x='170' y='152' class='lbl ink' text-anchor='middle'>1</text><text x='230' y='152' class='lbl ink' text-anchor='middle'>0</text><text x='180' y='190' class='sub ink' text-anchor='middle'>3x3 example: diagonal=0, off-diag cancel => sum=0</text></svg>
understand.visual_alt: 3x3 matrix with v[i][j]=i-j: diagonal entries are 0 (highlighted), off-diagonal entries appear in cancelling pairs.

given.aim: Compute the total sum of all elements of an n x n matrix v[i][j] = i - j.

terms.meaning: Each entry equals its row index minus its column index
When i = j, v[i][i] = i - i = 0

terms.connects: Every off-diagonal pair cancels, total sum = 0

given.plan: Compute sum = sum_{i=1}^{n} sum_{j=1}^{n} (i-j). Separate into two sums: sum of i over all j (= n * sum_i(i)) minus sum of j over all i (= n * sum_j(j)). Both equal n * n*(n+1)/2, so they cancel.

steps.title: Separate the double sum
steps.formula_raw: S = sum_{i=1}^{n} sum_{j=1}^{n} (i - j) = sum_{i,j} i - sum_{i,j} j
steps.apply: S = [sum_{i=1}^{n} sum_{j=1}^{n} i] - [sum_{i=1}^{n} sum_{j=1}^{n} j]. First part: for each fixed i, summing i over j=1..n gives n*i. So total = n * sum_{i=1}^{n} i = n * n(n+1)/2. Second part: for each fixed i (n rows), summing j=1..n gives n(n+1)/2. Over all n rows: n * n(n+1)/2.
steps.note: Both parts are identical by symmetry of i and j.

steps.title: Compute the total
steps.note: Alternatively: antisymmetric matrix always has zero sum. Each entry (i,j) cancels with (j,i), and diagonal is 0.

questions.formula_note: Antisymmetric matrix property: v[i][j] = -v[j][i] implies total sum = 0; equivalently sum_{i,j}(i-j) = n*sum(i) - n*sum(j) = 0 by symmetry.
